title: ophrys fusca subsp. fabrella
intro: Ophrys fusca subsp. fabrella, also known as the Bee Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a laxative due to its high content of alkaloids. The health benefits of Ophrys fusca subsp. fabrella include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, relieve constipation, and aid in the treatment of various gastrointestinal disorders. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and grinding the roots and leaves, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
